characters.name: Narrator
characters.persona: A warm, low male storyteller in his fifties, calm and cinematic, an unhurried documentary narrator.

characters.name: Mara
characters.persona: A weathered woman in her sixties, a lifelong lighthouse keeper. Low, gravelly, steady voice; firm and commanding, never rattled.

characters.name: Tomas
characters.persona: A young man in his late teens, a nervous apprentice. Higher-pitched, slightly breathy, anxious and eager to please.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The lighthouse at Karsk had not gone dark in ninety years.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Tonight, the storm meant to change that.

lines.speaker: Mara
lines.text: Bolt the door, Tomas. That wind will tear it off its hinges.

lines.speaker: Tomas
lines.text: I'm trying! The latch won't hold!

lines.speaker: Mara
lines.text: Then lean into it, boy. With me. On three.

lines.speaker: Mara
lines.text: The lamp's gone out. We light it by hand, or ships go down tonight.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: High above them, the great glass lens waited in the dark.

lines.speaker: Tomas
lines.text: I can't even see the wick.

lines.speaker: Mara
lines.text: You don't need to see it. You need to trust it. Strike the match.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: And in that small, stubborn flame, the whole coast found its way home.
